title: Uniface Installation Directory Structure
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Microsoft Windows
text: Uniface Installation Directory Structure The Uniface product directory contains the common and uniface main subdirectories. The Uniface main subdirectories include the following content: common —includes common components, executable and binary files, configuration files, and a single Tomcat web server. uniface —contain all files that are specific to the individual product, including components, utilities and configuration files. The /common and /uniface directories contain a number of subdirectories, such as: adm —initialization and assignment files that set the environment context for each application. components —application components sql —SQL scripts for creating Repository tables in supported databases usys —UAR files containing the runtime components required for deployment webapps —virtual root directory for the product's web applications. The Tomcat web server (located in /common ) is configured to use these directory locations. On Windows, the default location of the data directory is C:\Users\ UserAccount \Documents\ Rocket® Uniface Edition . It contains the data outputs created with the Uniface IDE and servers, including the Repository, compiled components, log files, and other files required by your application. A number of these directories are specified in predefined USYS path logicals, ensuring that each Uniface product or application can locate the files appropriate to its environment.
